_README: Cortex hooks for Claude Code. Copy the 'hooks' object below into your ~/.claude/settings.json or project-level .claude/settings.json. Alternatively, just use INSTRUCTIONS.md in your CLAUDE.md — hooks are optional and only make auto-recall more reliable.

hooks.command: echo '[cortex] Session ending — ensure observations were committed to ~/Documents/Claude/memory/'

_setup.step_2: Copy the 'hooks' object above and merge it into your settings file
_setup.step_3: Add INSTRUCTIONS.md content to your CLAUDE.md (this is required — hooks alone are not enough)
_setup.step_5: Start a new Claude Code session
_setup.minimal_setup: If you don't want hooks, just add INSTRUCTIONS.md content to ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md. The always-on behaviors are instruction-driven and work without hooks.
